title: Quaternion Lifting for Multichannel RF and Sensing Signals: A Canonical Lane Representation for Quaternionic Signal Processing
abstract: This paper defines a canonical quaternion lifting procedure for multichannel RF and sensing signals whose external intake remains standard complex-channel data. Given two complex channels z1[n]=a[n]+ib[n] and z2[n]=c[n]+id[n], the lift forms a quaternion lane q[n]=z1[n]+z2[n]j = a[n]+b[n]i+c[n]j+d[n]k. For larger captures, channels are grouped deterministically into quaternion lanes Q[g,n] in H. The paper formalizes this lift, its inverse unpacking map, odd-channel handling, and representation-level invariants that make it suitable as a canonical internal form. The contribution is representational rather than speculative: the lift preserves paired phase-bearing relations inside a single quaternion object while remaining exactly recoverable back to grouped complex channels. This establishes a deterministic, information-preserving front end for downstream quaternionic signal processing.
pdf_url: papers/qsp-001-canonical-quaternion-lift/1. Quaternion Lifting for Multichannel RF and Sensing Signals.pdf
notes: Draft manuscript now includes formal definitions, recoverability results, determinism, and energy-preservation invariants for the canonical grouped quaternion lift representation layer.
